title: 10 best herbal creams for chronic hepatitis

remedy_properties: Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant to reduce liver damage and inflammation, thereby improving liver function and overall health.
Flavonoids: Help protect the liver from further damage by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing oxidative stress.
Silipide: Enhances the absorption and bioavailability of silymarin, increasing its effectiveness in treating chronic hepatitis by delivering it directly to the liver tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, creams helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a bioactive compound that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin helps to protect liver cells from damage, reduce oxidative stress, and promote liver regeneration. By reducing inflammation and promoting liver health, milk thistle creams can help to alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis, such as fatigue, jaundice, and abdominal pain, improving overall quality of life for individuals with this condition.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: This flavonoid complex helps with chronic hepatitis by protecting liver cells from damage, reducing inflammation, and promoting liver regeneration.

Isosilybin: As a component of silymarin, isosilybin helps to scavenge free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, all of which contribute to liver protection and recovery.

Silychristin: This saponin constituent of silymarin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, promoting a healthier liver environment and facilitating the healing process.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps protect liver cells and promote liver regeneration.
Flowers: Contain silymarin and other flavonoids that aid in liver health and regeneration, as well as anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that support liver function and have antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves at dawn when they are fully open.
Dry the plant material in a warm place with air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Use a coffee grinder to finely grind 2 tablespoons of dried silybum marianum flowers.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the ground plant material with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to infuse in the double boiler for 2 hours then strain and bottle the cream.

remedy_properties: Choleretic and cholagogue effects: Dandelion creams stimulate the production of bile, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and improve digestion, ultimately alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion cream have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the liver from further damage, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.

Detoxification support: Dandelion root has been traditionally used to support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help remove toxins and excess bile acids that may accumulate in the liver due to chronic hepatitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb's roots and leaves contain sesquiterpene lactones and inulin, which have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting liver regeneration, dandelion cream may help alleviate symptoms and slow the progression of chronic hepatitis, making it a promising natural remedy for those suffering from this condition. Its benefits are attributed to its ability to detoxify and nourish the liver.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, helps reduce liver inflammation and promotes the excretion of bile, which can aid in the treatment of chronic hepatitis by alleviating liver congestion and improving liver function.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties and has been shown to inhibit liver fibrosis, a common complication of chronic hepatitis, by reducing the accumulation of collagen and promoting the degradation of scar tissue.

Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help mitigate liver damage and promote the healing of liver cells in chronic hepatitis patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in saponins, which may help reduce inflammation and liver damage.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids, which may help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration.
Roots: Contain sesquiterpene lactones, which may help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried taraxacum officinale root and 250ml of vegetable oil in a clean room.
Combine the dried root with 100ml of glycerin and 20ml of distilled water in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture at 60-70°c for 3 hours while gently stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Whip 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil mixture until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress caused by chronic hepatitis.
Flavonoids: Have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help combat the viral infection and modulate the immune response to alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
Licoricidin: Has anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic effects, which can help reduce liver fibrosis (scarring) caused by chronic hepatitis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which exacerbate liver inflammation. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to have antioxidant properties, which help protect liver cells from oxidative damage. The soothing and calming effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may also help reduce liver inflammation, making it an effective adjunctive treatment for chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin (Glycyrrhizin): This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce liver inflammation, which is beneficial in managing chronic hepatitis.

Liquiritigenin (Liquiritin): This isoflavan is known for its antioxidant activity, which helps to neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells and contribute to the progression of chronic hepatitis.

Licoflavonoids (Glycyrrhizic acid): These compounds have been shown to have anti-viral properties, which can help to reduce the viral load in patients with chronic hepatitis, thereby slowing disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hepatitis.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that contribute to their hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, making them suitable for treating chronic hepatitis.
Roots: They also contain glycosides and triterpenoids that have been shown to have immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects, which can help in managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root with 500ml of 95% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Filter the resulting liquid and discard the solids through a 0.2 micron filter.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 100ml of the filtered glycyrrhiza glabra extract to the melted wax mixture.
Stir the mixture thoroughly and pour it into a container for 30 minutes of cooling and solidification.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas like milk thistle, turmeric, and ginger have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like chamomile, peppermint, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in people with chronic hepatitis.

Immune-modulating properties: Herbal teas like echinacea and astragalus contain immune-boosting compounds that may enhance the body's natural defenses against viral hepatitis, such as Hepatitis B and C.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in Camellia sinensis creams, such as theaflavins and thearubigins, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying causes of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, these flavonoids may also help to improve liver function and promote the regeneration of liver cells, making Camellia sinensis creams a promising natural remedy for managing chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which are common symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Catechins: Catechins, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect liver tissue.
Buds: Utilized for their potential hepatoprotective effects and ability to stimulate liver regeneration.

remedy_recipe: Steep 2 teaspoons of camellia sinensis leaves in 8 ounces of hot water for 3 to 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to remove solids.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Mix the camellia sinensis liquid with the cooled mixture from step 4 and pour into a container to cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Phyllanthus amarus cream has been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis B virus (HBV) and reduce viral load, helping to control the progression of chronic hepatitis.

Immune modulatory effects: The cream's immunomodulatory properties can help regulate an overactive immune response, which is often associated with chronic liver inflammation and damage caused by HBV infection.

Antioxidant activity: Phyllanthus amarus cream's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and liver damage caused by free radicals, which are often elevated in patients with chronic hepatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh phyllanthus amarus leaves and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Extract the essential oil from 250 grams of the cleaned leaves using a solvent like ethanol in a ratio of 1:2.
Mix the extracted oil with 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 20 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of a natural preservative like potassium sorbate to the mixture.
Stir the mixture continuously for 15 minutes and then pour it into containers for solidification and storage.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric cream's cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibitory activity helps reduce the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids, which can contribute to liver damage and fibrosis in chronic hepatitis patients.

Immunomodulatory effects: The immunomodulatory compounds present in turmeric creams help modulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced immune function that is beneficial for managing chronic hepatitis.

Bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids: They help with chronic hepatitis by exerting anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, thereby reducing liver inflammation and modulating the immune response to fight the disease.

remedy_recipe: Extract the root of berberis aristata from the plant using a solvent like ethanol at a ratio of 1:4.
Filter the root extract to remove impurities and obtain a clear solution within 30 minutes.
Combine the filtered extract with a base like coconut oil or shea butter in a ratio of 1:10.
Add a preservative like potassium sorbate to the mixture to prevent spoilage within 15 minutes.
Emulsify the mixture and fill it into containers for storage at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Chebulic Myrobalan cream's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells and contribute to chronic hepatitis progression, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

remedy_parts: Fruits: Used due to their high content of chebulagic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing liver inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried terminalia chebula using 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Combine the herbal extract with 200 grams of shea butter and the wax-oil mixture in a separate saucepan.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound curcumin reduces liver inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis such as pain, fatigue, and joint stiffness.
Antioxidant properties: Curcumin's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals in the body that can contribute to liver damage and disease progression in chronic hepatitis patients.

Immunomodulatory properties: Turmeric's immunoregulatory effects may help to modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver while also supporting its natural defense mechanisms against viral and bacterial infections that can exacerbate chronic hepatitis.

By inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, curcumin helps to alleviate liver damage and promote healing. remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound in Curcuma longa, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which contribute to liver damage in chronic hepatitis.

Turmerone (α-Turmerone): Turmerone, a sesquiterpene in Curcuma longa, has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help protect the liver from damage caused by chronic hepatitis and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain curcumin, a polyphenol compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing liver inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are rich in curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried turmeric root, 50 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of coconut oil, 10 grams of shea butter, 10 grams of vitamin e oil, and 5 grams of essential oil.
Melt 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 50-60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of dried turmeric root to 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture from step 3 through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove the solids for 5 minutes.
Mix the strained mixture from step 4 with 20 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of vitamin e oil, and 5 grams of essential oil in the double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in ginger cream, like vitamin C and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells and contribute to chronic hepatitis, thereby protecting the liver from further harm.

remedy_recipe: Infuse zingiber officinale roots in 1 liter of distilled water at 82 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid.
Mix 250 milliliters of aloe vera gel with the infused liquid in a stainless steel bowl.
Add 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently until the wax dissolves.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let them cool at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, can help reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh cichorium intybus leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Combine the harvested material with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil for 10 minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a saucepan and heat gently until the beeswax melts.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well before pouring into jars.

remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers, drying them thoroughly within 24 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 250 grams of dried althaea officinalis plant material with 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it twice daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20 milliliters of the althaea officinalis infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and heat it gently in a double boiler until melted and fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and flowers at dawn when they are most potent for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or oven drying at 30°c for 24 hours to preserve properties.
Infuse dried plant material in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10:1 for 2 weeks with occasional stirring to extract active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 20% concentration for thickening and stability.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify into a cream consistency, which can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in aloe vera, including vitamins C and E, beta-carotene, and other polyphenols, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to liver cell damage and scarring, thereby protecting the liver from further harm.

remedy_recipe: Gather aloe vera gel extract 200mg and combine it with coconut oil 20g.
Mix the aloe vera gel extract with beeswax 10g and shea butter 20g thoroughly.
Add licorice root powder 5g and dandelion root powder 5g to the mixture stir well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low temperature for 30 minutes until smooth consistency.
Fill sterile containers with the cream and refrigerate it for at least 24 hours before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhus aromatica leaves and dry them for at least 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind dried rhus aromatica leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, requiring approximately 10 minutes of grinding time.
Combine the powdered rhus aromatica with 20% beeswax and 30% coconut oil by weight, heating the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 1 hour to melt the wax.

Remove the mixture from heat and add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 0.5 teaspoons of tea tree essential oil, stirring until well combined and allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain a unique combination of natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. Additionally, the cream's emollient base provides soothing relief from itching and discomfort associated with chronic hepatitis, allowing patients to experience improved quality of life and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Guggulu's anti-inflammatory property: Guggulu cream helps reduce inflammation in the liver, which is a hallmark feature of chronic hepatitis, thereby alleviating symptoms and promoting healing.

Guggulu's antioxidant property: The antioxidant activity of guggulu cream helps scavenge free radicals that damage liver cells, protecting against oxidative stress and preventing further liver damage.

Guggulu's immunomodulatory property: Guggulu cream has been shown to modulate the immune response in chronic hepatitis patients, reducing inflammation and promoting a balanced immune response that helps combat viral infections.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Guggul contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of commiphora mukul resin in a well-ventilated area for 2 days.
Grind the dried resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered resin with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax at 65 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally until it thickens and reaches a consistency suitable for cream, taking about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation processes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Potato creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as saponins and flavonoids, work to reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with the disease. Barks: The barks of the potato plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help protect the liver cells from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers from your garden or store.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers with 1 tablespoon of potato starch in a mortar and pestle.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder for approximately 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of distilled water to the powdered mixture.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly and store the cream in an airtight container for up to 7 days.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Some herbs like turmeric, ginger, and calendula may be used in topical creams or ointments to help reduce inflammation in the liver, which can exacerbate chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant properties: Herbs rich in antioxidants like green tea, ginkgo biloba, and licorice root may be used to help protect the liver from further damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain adaptogenic properties that soothe and calm the liver, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ashwagandha cream's adaptogenic property helps to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which is common in individuals with chronic hepatitis, allowing the body to respond better to the condition.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ashwagandha cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the liver, which can further damage liver tissue and exacerbate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

remedy_parts: Roots: Ashwagandha roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat liver disorders, including chronic hepatitis, due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.

Barks: The barks of the ashwagandha plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ashwagandha roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered ashwagandha with 200 grams of petroleum jelly and 100 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at 150°f for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: South American yerba creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of their unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties. remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Barks: Contain phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against liver damage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achyrocline satureioides leaves and dry them in shade at room temperature for two weeks to preserve potency.
Grind dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through 100-mesh sieve to remove large particles.
Combine powdered achyrocline satureioides with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in double boiler, stirring until mixture reaches temperature of 160°f.
Continue heating mixture for additional hour to enhance absorption and penetration properties, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Whip cooled mixture with hand blender or whisk until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterilized containers and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, blueberry barks have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiviral properties, making them effective against viral hepatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend the rinsed berries with 200ml of distilled water in a blender or food processor for 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a large bowl to extract the juice and pulp from the berries.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the vaccinium myrtillus juice in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into sterilized glass jars and refrigerating at 4°c.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for chronic hepatitis are topical treatments that combine herbal extracts to provide relief from symptoms of chronic hepatitis, such as liver inflammation and damage. They help by reducing inflammation, promoting liver regeneration, and improving overall liver function. The best herbal creams for chronic hepatitis include Silybum marianum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Phyllanthus niruri, Andrographis paniculata, Cynara scolymus, Artemisia absinthium, Taraxacum officinale, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, chronic hepatitis can lead to liver cirrhosis, liver failure, and even death, significantly impacting one's quality of life, causing fatigue, weight loss, and difficulty performing daily tasks.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic hepatitis that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and acne. These conditions share similarities with chronic hepatitis in terms of inflammation, skin lesions, and oxidative stress. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and turmeric have been shown to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with chronic hepatitis is a blend of Turmeric-based cream, containing curcumin, which reduces inflammation and oxidative stress. Adding a cream with Milk Thistle, rich in silymarin, supports liver function and regeneration. A cream infused with Dandelion root, which stimulates liver detoxification, and Echinacea, which boosts the immune system, completes the combination. This synergy of herbs can potentially aid in managing chronic hepatitis symptoms and promoting liver health. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain silymarin, a bioactive compound extracted from the milk thistle plant. Silymarin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect liver cells from damage and promote their regeneration. This can lead to improved liver function and reduced inflammation in individuals with chronic hepatitis. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, silymarin may also help to prevent the progression of liver disease and improve overall liver health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: This complex mixture of flavonoids has antioxidant properties, which help protect liver cells from oxidative damage and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Isosilybin: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, isosilybin has been shown to inhibit liver fibrosis, a common complication of chronic hepatitis.
Silibinin: This flavonoid has been found to possess anti-viral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce liver damage and improve liver function in patients with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high silymarin content, which is the primary bioactive compound responsible for the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects beneficial for treating chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: They are used due to their rich silymarin content, which provides similar benefits as the seeds, making them a viable alternative for treating chronic hepatitis.
Fruits: They are used due to their silymarin content, which helps in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby aiding in the treatment of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves and dry them further for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered silybum marianum with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 180-200 degrees fahrenheit for 30-40 minutes or until the beeswax has melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken for 30-60 minutes before transferring it to a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams help with chronic hepatitis because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Glycyrrhizin has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to have hepatoprotective effects, protecting liver cells from damage and promoting liver regeneration. As a result, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression in chronic hepatitis patients.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with chronic hepatitis by inhibiting the expression of inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing liver inflammation.
Licopyroside: This glycoside flavonoid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce liver damage and promote liver regeneration in chronic hepatitis.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to protect liver cells from oxidative damage, reduce liver inflammation, and promote liver regeneration in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce liver inflammation and promote liver health.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties and help to protect the liver from damage.
Roots: They also contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to soothe liver irritation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 500g of distilled water and 250g of glycerin in a stainless steel container.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a stainless steel spoon.
Add 100g of finely powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the mixture and stir for 10 minutes.
Use a homogenizer to mix the cream at 10,000 rpm for 5 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.
Fill 100ml glass jars with the cream and store them in a refrigerator at 4-8°c for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the root causes of liver inflammation. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting antioxidant defenses, curcumin has been shown to improve liver function and reduce inflammation in patients with chronic hepatitis. Additionally, curcumin's anti-fibrotic properties may help prevent scarring and fibrosis in the liver, leading to improved liver health and potentially even remission in some cases.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, thereby alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Volatile oils (specifically, Turmerone and Atlantone): These terpenoids have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce liver damage and promote healing in chronic hepatitis.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This curcuminoid has been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to liver damage and fibrosis in chronic hepatitis, thereby helping to reduce disease progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate hepatitis symptoms.
Stems: Stems contain curcuminoids, which have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are used in creams for chronic hepatitis due to their rich content of curcumin, which has been found to improve liver function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 3 tablespoons of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the turmeric powder with 1 tablespoon of honey and 2 tablespoons of the beeswax mixture in a bowl.
Whisk the mixture constantly while slowly adding 2 ounces of distilled water over 5 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress. These compounds also exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate pathogens that may be contributing to the condition. Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids have potent antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals contributing to liver damage and fibrosis in chronic hepatitis.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in regulating the immune response and reducing liver damage in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used because they contain a rich source of gingerols and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used for their high content of volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of zingiber officinale rhizomes in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Combine 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler and melt over low heat.
Add 50g of the infused oil and 5g of vitamin e oil to the melted wax mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus niruri creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This herbal remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat liver-related issues, including hepatitis. Triterpenoids: Contribute to alleviating chronic hepatitis by possessing hepatoprotective properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and protect liver cells from damage.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help in treating liver diseases.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves of the phyllanthus niruri plant in the early morning.
Dry the fresh leaves in a warm oven at 50°c for 6-8 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring constantly, until smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain the anti-inflammatory compound andrographolide, which has been shown to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. Neoverrucoside: This phenolic glycoside exhibits antioxidant activity, protecting liver cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Dihydro-andrographolide: This labdane diterpenoid compound has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune response and reduce liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Andrographis paniculata leaves are widely used due to their high concentration of andrographolide, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate liver damage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers from the plant in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind 200 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax with the ground plant powder in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes or until the mixture thickens.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which work synergistically to reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. The artichoke extract in Cynara scolymus creams has been shown to improve liver function by reducing liver enzymes and enhancing bile production, ultimately leading to improved liver health and reduced symptoms of chronic hepatitis. remedy_constituents: Silymarin: This flavonoid complex helps protect the liver from damage, reduce inflammation, and promote regeneration of liver cells, contributing to improved liver function in chronic hepatitis.

Asteridiol: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Lignan glycosides: These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce liver damage, inflammation, and oxidative stress in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cynara scolymus leaves are rich in antioxidants and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties.
Buds: Cynara scolymus buds contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help protect the liver and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: Cynara scolymus flowers are a source of saponins and other bioactive compounds that may have anti-hepatotoxic and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cynara scolymus flowers on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested cynara scolymus flowers in a warm place for 7-10 days to remove moisture.
Create a decoction by mixing 50g of dried cynara scolymus flowers with 500ml of water in a saucepan.
Simmer the decoction over low heat for 10-15 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Mix the cooled decoction with 100g of beeswax and 200ml of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as absinthin and artabsin, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common characteristics of chronic hepatitis. remedy_constituents: Artabsin (sesquiterpene lactone): It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Thujone (alkylate sesquiterpene): It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration.
Caffeic acid (phenolic compound): It has antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the liver, thereby aiding in the management of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties that help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
Roots: They possess bioactive compounds like absinthin, which has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in treating chronic hepatitis.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to have hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties, contributing to their use in creams for chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves and flowers of artemisia absinthium in the early morning to ensure peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to prevent degradation.
Infuse 20 grams of dried plant material in 100 milliliters of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks to extract active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal extract.
Combine the herbal extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells from damage. This is particularly beneficial for individuals with chronic hepatitis, as it can help alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression. Additionally, Taraxacum officinale has been traditionally used to support liver function and promote overall digestive health. remedy_constituents: Inulin: Helps by promoting the regeneration of liver cells and improving liver function, thus aiding in the treatment of chronic hepatitis.
Taraxasterol: Assists in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which are common factors contributing to chronic hepatitis.
Fatty acid derivatives (e.g., taraxasterol esters): May help in reducing liver damage and promoting the healing of liver tissue by modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for liver health.
Roots: Utilized for their rich content of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective effects.
Flowers: Employed for their high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate liver damage.
remedy_recipe: Extract taraxacum officinale roots by steeping 100g of dried roots in 500ml of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids immediately.
Combine the taraxacum officinale extract with 200g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally until all the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce liver inflammation, improve blood flow to the liver, and protect liver cells from damage. As a result, ginkgo biloba creams may alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis, such as fatigue, nausea, and loss of appetite. They may also enhance liver function, promote detoxification, and support overall liver health, making them a potential adjunctive therapy for managing chronic hepatitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to chronic hepatitis.
Bilobalide: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration, which can aid in the treatment of chronic hepatitis.

Ginkgotoxin: Although it can have toxic effects at high concentrations, at therapeutic doses, it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and damage, which can be beneficial for chronic hepatitis patients.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because of their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting healing.
Seeds: They are used for their potential to reduce oxidative stress and improve liver function, contributing to the management of chronic hepatitis.
Barks: They are used due to their flavonoid content, which has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to protect the liver from damage.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 100ml of jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 10ml of vitamin e oil and 10ml of coconut oil.
Combine the infused ginkgo biloba oil with the beeswax mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.
